title: Level 770
wikitext:
{{CSS|content= .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0,0.0); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgba(0, 0, 0,0.0); --theme-page-text-color: #FFF; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: #000000; --theme-community-header-color: #FFFFFF; --theme-link-color: #999; --theme-body-dynamic-color-1: #fff; --theme-page-dynamic-color-1: #fff; } .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0,0.0); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgba(0, 0, 0,0.0); --theme-page-text-color: #FFF; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: #000000; --theme-community-header-color: #FFFFFF; --theme-link-color: #999; --theme-body-dynamic-color-1: #fff; --theme-page-dynamic-color-1: #fff; } }} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 770: "Blacklight Hospital"}}
{{CSS|content=.backgroundplate { filter: blur(12px); }}} {{Background|file=766_1.jpg}}
{{Class 5|entity=Presence of Entities Unconfirmed|security=Unknown Information|safety=Mysterious Properties|class=Undetermined|hexagon=?|color-s=0%}} '''Level 770''' is the 771st level of The Backrooms.
'''Description'''
{{Blurimage|file=766_1.jpg|caption=The first documented picture of '''Level 770'''.}} '''Level 770''', otherwise known as '''"Blacklight Hospital"''' manifests as an expansive late-2000s to early-2010s metropolitan hospital complex of indeterminate size. The level is characterized by an ultraviolet illumination system, with the overwhelming majority of visible light originating from UV ceiling fixtures, emergency wall strips, exposed maintenance housings, and diagnostic equipment. Conventional fluorescent ceiling lights are present throughout the facility, however, they cannot be turned on in any way. In the rare instances where standard lighting is operational, it is typically confined to elevator interiors or isolated maintenance rooms. Ambient temperature within '''Level 770''' consistently ranges between 18°C and 22°C (64.4°F-71.6°F), while airflow is continuously present despite the lack of identifiable ventilation systems. The level is also abnormally clean, as everything is entirely devoid of dust, rust, stains, or biological residue. Despite this, no custodial staff or autonomous maintenance systems have ever been observed. Food sources are absent throughout the level, however, all tested tap water appears to be drinkable. The level contains nearly all departments expected within a large-scale medical institution. Informational signage and wall-mounted directories are present throughout the facility, however, some directories reference departments, medical disciplines, or treatment wings with no known equivalent in conventional medicine. Windows aren’t frequent, but appear throughout higher floors and select stairwells. All of them are completely blacked out and cannot be opened or damaged by any documented means. No environmental features beyond the windows have been observed, and audio recordings taken near these windows reveal no exterior sound. All text within '''Level 770''' appears in a different random language from [[The Frontrooms]]. Most documentation resembles authentic hospital paperwork involving anatomy, surgical procedures, or medical records, however, all patient identities are replaced with numerical identification strings rather than names. All timekeeping devices within the level display random, static times which do not progress naturally, and scattered throughout the complex, particularly on the seventh floor, are small framed paintings depicting skinless humanoid figures on black backgrounds. These figures possess flayed animal heads in place of human ones, most commonly those of dogs, deer, sheep, or foxes. The paintings exhibit no identifiable artistic signature and have shown resistance to physical removal attempts.
'''Floors'''
{{Blurimage|file=766_2.jpg|caption=A waiting area in '''Level 770''' with a visible monitor displaying Greek text.}} '''Level 770''' currently consists of twelve documented floors ''(7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2, Ground Floor, -1, -2, -3, -4, -5)'', with the existence of additional undocumented floors remaining possible. Architectural designs and lighting conditions remain highly consistent throughout all accessible sections of the level. '''Ground floor''' The Ground Floor serves as the primary reception and emergency intake area of '''Level 770'''. It contains a large central lobby with a reception desk, extensive waiting areas with rows of plastic seating, trauma rooms, triage stations, small storage units, a cafeteria, and multiple security offices. The floor is visually close to a standard hospital ground floor, though the entrance doors are not present within the lobby area nor anywhere else. Wanderers entering Level 770 with only minor injuries typically regain consciousness seated in a waiting area on this floor. '''2nd floor''' Floor 2 functions as the primary diagnostic wing. It contains MRI suites, CT scanning rooms, X-ray facilities, ultrasound laboratories, blood analysis stations, and numerous diagnostic offices. A constant, quiet, low electrical hum of inactive medical machinery can be heard on this floor. Diagnostic rooms frequently appear prepared for immediate patient use despite no personnel being present. Examination beds are often warm to the touch, and some computer monitors occasionally show incomplete scans of unidentified human anatomies inconsistent with known biology. '''3rd floor''' Floor 3 contains the majority of the level’s surgical infrastructure. The floor includes operating theaters, recovery rooms, sterilization chambers, surgical preparation units, and anesthesia storage facilities. Bright surgical lamps are suspended above operating tables, though they emit ultraviolet rather than white light. '''4th floor''' Floor 4 serves as the intensive care and critical treatment wing. It contains ICU wards, cardiac monitoring rooms, ventilator stations, and high-dependency patient units. The ambient audio environment on this floor is dominated by distant electronic beeping and occasional static interferences transmitted through unused intercom speakers. Curtained patient bays are lined in extensive corridors, and some cardiac monitors display stable heart rhythms despite having no visible patient connected. The ultraviolet lighting appears dimmer on this floor than elsewhere in the level. '''5th floor''' Floor 5 consists primarily of standard inpatient recovery wards used for long-term observation and recuperation. The floor contains rows of individual patient rooms, shared wards, nurse stations, medication storage areas, and rehabilitation equipment. Wanderers report feelings of unusual tiredness while present here. Individuals who enter '''Level 770''' with severe injuries regain consciousness in patient rooms on this floor, already having received extensive medical treatment. {{Blurimage|file=766_3.jpg|caption=A bed in one of the inpatient rooms located on the 5th floor.}} '''6th floor''' Floor 6 houses specialized treatment departments, including cardiology, neurology, oncology, dialysis, and various unidentified medical divisions. Department signage frequently references unfamiliar conditions or procedures not recognized by conventional medicine. Certain examination rooms contain medical equipment with no identifiable purpose. The floor also contains several sealed treatment wings accessible only through electronically locked doors which cannot be bypassed through conventional means. '''7th floor''' Floor 7 serves as the administrative and research division of the level. It contains executive offices, conference rooms, archival storage, medical records departments, and extensive research laboratories. It's the only floor with carpeting, which replaces portions of the tile flooring. Numerous mentioned framed paintings depicting skinless humanoid entities with flayed animal heads are mounted throughout corridors and offices. Research laboratories contain large quantities of documentation, preserved biological material, and surgical instruments. Several recovered documents reference experimental procedures involving concepts not recognized by modern medicine. '''Floor -1''' Floor -1 functions as the primary maintenance and support infrastructure level. It contains industrial laundry facilities, staff utility rooms, generator chambers, empty morgue refrigeration units, and maintenance tunnels. The lighting on this floor flickers occasionally, unlike on other floors in the level. '''Floor -2''' Floor -2 contains large-scale storage facilities, organ preservation chambers, medical waste processing systems, and refrigerated biological containment units. This floor possesses the coldest ambient temperature recorded within the level. '''Floor -3, -4, and -5''' These floors remain entirely inaccessible, as the stairwells leading to them are blocked with sealed security barriers, and elevator controls corresponding to these floors fail to respond when pressed. Audio recordings taken near access points occasionally capture distant metallic impacts or indistinct low-frequency sounds originating from beneath the barriers. No successful entry into these floors has been documented.
'''Anomalies'''
===Medical treatment=== Wanderers entering '''Level 770''' regain consciousness either in a medical bed or seated within a waiting area. Entry placement appears directly correlated with injury severity prior to entry, as individuals with serious trauma awaken in inpatient wards or surgical recovery rooms, while ones who suffered minor injuries find themselves in a waiting area. Every documented entrant has exhibited signs of recent medical intervention upon awakening, as their wounds have been medically repaired without any visible side effects. Despite this, no medical staff or living entities have ever been observed performing these procedures. No documented case exists of an uninjured individual successfully entering '''Level 770'''. Wanderers who hadn't eaten for a prolonged amount of time had reported awakening connected to IV drips delivering nutrients directly into the bloodstream via TPN treatment ===Surgeries=== Numerous wanderers report awakening with fresh surgical incisions or stitches despite possessing no memory of any operation taking place. Most individuals describe feeling physically improved afterward, regardless of the invasiveness of the apparent procedure. A minority of reports describe experiences consistent with conscious paralysis during surgery, wherein subjects claimed to faintly perceive bodily sensations or movement while remaining unable to open their eyes or move voluntarily. One wanderer exhibited severe signs of psychological trauma after exiting the level and later reported that they felt pain during surgery. ===Broadcasts=== The intercom system within '''Level 770''' can activate intermittently without warning. Activations are typically preceded by a short electronic chime transmitted through wall-mounted speakers. Most reported broadcasts consist of distorted biological noises, wet impacts, breathing, or indecipherable vocalizations lasting several minutes before abruptly stopping. One wanderer reported hearing a distorted electronic melody that progressively slowed to an unnaturally low pitch before cutting out entirely. Another described a silent activation in which the speakers emitted only static electrical feedback.
'''Entities'''
No entities have ever been documented within '''Level 770'''.
'''Colonies & Outposts'''
None exist within '''Level 770''' due to its nature.
'''Entrances and Exits'''
===Entrances=== * Locating an elevator which has a small rectangular button with a blue glowing medical cross symbol rather than conventional floor indicators, and then pressing the button while injured. If one is uninjured while doing so, the elevator doors will not close and nothing will happen. So far, it has only been rarely seen appear in [[Level 66]], [[Level 154]], [[Level 188]], [[Level 297]], and [[Level 504]]. * Getting severely injured on any level has an extremely small chance to send one to '''Level 770'''. ===Exits=== * No method of intentionally exiting '''Level 770''' has been discovered. It is currently presumed that the level releases individuals autonomously once they are considered medically stabilized or no longer requiring treatment. In currently confirmed exits, wanderers regained consciousness in [[Level 427]] and on very rare occasions [[Level 45]] or [[Level 100]].
'''Additional Information'''
On May 16th, 2019, [[M.E.G.]] explorers (Dorian Thévenet (29) and Miłosz Wolar (34)) initiated the first documented exploration of '''Level 770''' with the objective of mapping the level and collecting environmental data. After locating the elevator within [[Level 154]], both individuals inflicted a shallow cut on their forearm in order to satisfy the entrance conditions before activating the elevator mechanism. The following information has been reconstructed from recovered audio recordings later retrieved during Expedition 3.
Expedition 1
'''Day 1'''
{{Blurimage|file=766_4.jpg|caption=A picture of a hallway in '''Level 770''' found on Miłosz's recovered phone.}} The first recording begins with Miłosz Wolar regaining consciousness inside a waiting area located on the Ground Floor. He reports no memory of events occurring after the elevator doors closed. His self-inflicted wound had already been professionally bandaged upon awakening. Miłosz initially describes significant visual disorientation caused by the ultraviolet lighting conditions, noting that without a flashlight he was unable to perceive accurate coloration within the environment. He proceeded to explore nearby sections of the floor, documenting reception areas, trauma rooms, hallways, and multiple vacant waiting areas. Approximately 52 minutes into the recording, Miłosz reunited with Dorian Thévenet, who had awakened elsewhere within the level under similar circumstances. The two explorers subsequently conducted a coordinated survey of the Ground Floor, completing preliminary mapping efforts approximately 1 hour and 21 minutes into the expedition. Dorian later reported locating a cafeteria area fully stocked with utensils and food preparation equipment, though no consumable food items were present. Following completion of the Ground Floor survey, the explorers advanced to Floors 2 and 3, documenting diagnostic departments, operating theaters, and recovery wards. By approximately 4 hours and 17 minutes into the recording archive, both floors had been extensively explored and verbally described. Dorian was reportedly producing physical maps of each floor layout during exploration, however, these materials were never recovered. As exploration continued, both individuals began exhibiting signs of extreme fatigue disproportionate to their physical activity. The explorers elected to rest overnight within a smaller empty recovery room located on Floor 3.
'''Day 2'''
During the second day, Miłosz and Dorian spent an estimated 13 hours surveying Floors 4 through 7 as well as Floors -1 and -2. The majority of '''Level 770'''’s currently accepted visual documentation originates from Miłosz’s recorded descriptions during this period. Both explorers confirmed that Floors -3 through -5 were inaccessible. Stairwell access ended at sealed barriers, while elevator controls corresponding to those floors failed to function. While exploring Floor -2, Dorian reportedly began exhibiting mild paranoia and expressed the sensation of being observed despite the complete absence of visible entities or movement. During the latter half of the exploration, the intercom system activated twice. In both cases, the speakers emitted the standard electronic chime but no discernible message followed. Having concluded that the accessible areas of the level had been mapped, the explorers decided to begin searching for an exit the following day. They traveled to Floor 5 via elevator and rested within one of the inpatient recovery rooms.
'''Day 3'''
The first recording from Day 3 begins with Miłosz reporting that he had awakened connected to an IV drip delivering fluids directly into his bloodstream despite possessing no memory of any medical procedure occurring during the night. He immediately removed the IV apparatus. He additionally stated that he no longer experienced sensations of hunger or thirst and felt physically healthier than during the previous day. Dorian reported a separate incident involving severe chest discomfort upon awakening. Upon examination, he found fresh surgical stitches across his thoracic region consistent with a recent invasive operation. Dorian possessed no memory of undergoing surgery. Both explorers expressed distress regarding these developments and immediately began attempting to locate an exit from the level. Only one additional recording from Day 3 exists. In it, Miłosz states that the pair had spent approximately 14 continuous hours searching unsuccessfully for an exit. Due to the unexplained medical procedures experienced during their previous sleep, both explorers refused to remain overnight on Floor 5 again, and instead established a temporary resting position inside a small Ground Floor security office.
'''Day 4'''
Unlike previous recordings, the Day 4 audio log appears to have been started some time after awakening and not immediately after. Miłosz reports that upon waking, he discovered Dorian missing from the security room. All of Dorian’s belongings remained untouched, and no evidence of struggle was observed. Miłosz initially theorized that Dorian may have wandered away independently. Throughout the recording, Miłosz demonstrates visible unease. He states his intention to search the level for Dorian before ending the recording. A later recording documents Miłosz reporting that several hours of searching had yielded no evidence regarding Dorian’s location or condition. He states that he intends to return to the security office to rest and resume searching the following day if Dorian does not come back.
'''Day 5 & 6'''
No recordings from Days 5 or 6 are present.
'''Day 7'''
The final recovered recording from Expedition 1 is approximately 31 seconds in length. The audio consists primarily of rapid breathing accompanied by Miłosz repeating the phrase ''“I need to get out of here”'' in his native language. His voice is strained, unstable, and significantly quieter than in previous recordings. A faint cardiac monitor can be heard operating somewhere nearby in the background. No further recordings or communications from Miłosz Wolar had been found. Following the disappearance of both explorers, Dorian Thévenet and Miłosz Wolar were officially classified as missing by the [[M.E.G.]]
Fourteen days after entering '''Level 770''', Dorian was unexpectedly located in [[Level 427]] near the established [[M.E.G.]] Base φ. Witnesses described him as displaying extensive surgical scarring across the chest, abdomen, neck, and portions of the face. Personnel additionally noted visible severe psychological distress consistent with extreme trauma. Before anyone could question him, Dorian swiftly seized a firearm from a nearby member and fatally shot himself in the head. Due to this, no information regarding his experiences within the level was obtained. === Expedition 2 === A second exploration team has later entered '''Level 770'''. The expedition produced minimal additional information, though all members ultimately exited the level alive. One explorer emerged only two hours after entry with his original self-inflicted wound professionally bandaged and no additional anomalies present. The remaining two members exited approximately three days later. One, who had previously possesed a bilateral cataract in his left eye, had it surgically removed with extreme precision despite the absence of any visible postoperative complications. The other explorer exhibited a large fresh incision extending vertically across the spine. Both individuals had no memories of any surgical procedures occurring, though they reported feeling somewhat physically healthier upon exit. === Expedition 3 === Expedition 3 successfully recovered Miłosz Wolar’s mobile phone along with the complete archive of Expedition 1 audio recordings. All members exited Level 770 after approximately six days, though each individual left separately over the course of several hours. The final member to exit displayed a severe surgical incision extending from the collarbone to the lower abdomen. He reported faintly recalling temporary conscious paralysis accompanied by sensations of internal manipulation, though he was uncertain whether the experience had been real or dreamlike. During the expedition, personnel additionally recovered a preserved organic tissue from one of the organ storage chambers on Floor -2. The tissue appeared to be a section of human large intestine approximately equal in length to a human forearm. Later biological analysis confirmed that the tissue belonged to Miłosz Wolar. No additional remains were recovered, and Miłosz’s fate remains unknown.
'''Addendum'''
Following the incident involving Miłosz Wolar and Dorian Thévenet, '''Level 770''' has been reclassified from a [https://backrooms.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Safe Safe Class] to [https://backrooms.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Class_Undetermined Class Undetermined]. {{Redirect|Prev=769|Next=771}} {{Collapse|initial=> Open Author & Licensing Information|final=< Close Author & Licensing Information|body=Original Author: [[User:Corthealis|Corthealis]]}} [[Category:Cluster I]] [[Category:Class Undetermined]] [[Category:Secret Levels]] [[Category:Mysterious]] [[Category:Horror]] [[Category:Levels]]
